hypothesis: We believe the HDD skill produces actionable hypotheses from messy real-world artifacts if practitioners submit PRDs, decks, and briefs and can get clear next steps (INK → PRE → test plan).
baseline: No baseline — this is the first test of the skill on real artifacts.
success_threshold: ≥3 of 5 test artifacts yield ≥1 hypothesis that the submitter confirms is more actionable than what they had before analysis
falsification_criteria: This hypothesis is FALSE if: (1) <3 of 5 artifacts yield actionable output, (2) users say they could have done this themselves without the skill, (3) generated hypotheses lack situation/motivation/outcome structure
outcome_notes: THRESHOLD MET: 5/5. Every artifact produced more specific testable output than existed before. Intake surfaced gaps invisible to original authors. LEARNING: Skill's weakest phase is Phase 5 output routing. Input contract catches structural gaps; output contract doesn't evaluate experiment design quality. NEXT: PRE-003.

hypothesis: We believe distinguishable frustration patterns are achievable if workers with daily AI tool use can get compared meaningfully to workers with minimal AI exposure using self-reported journal entries.
baseline: No baseline — pilot required to establish whether cohort-specific themes emerge at all.
success_threshold: Cohen's kappa ≥0.6 on cohort-specific frustration theme coding across two independent raters
falsification_criteria: This hypothesis is FALSE if: (1) Qualitative coding finds no consistent theme separation between AI-Heavy and Low-AI cohorts after N≥30 entries per cohort, (2) Inter-rater agreement on cohort assignment from blind journal review is no better than chance (κ<0.2), (3) Participants in AI-Heavy cohort cannot name specific tools or describe daily AI workflows when asked in follow-up
timeline: Pilot: 5 participants/cohort × 7 days. Full study contingent on pilot results.

hypothesis: We believe the skill's output contract can enforce experiment design validity if teams generating hypotheses can get explicit challenge criteria for whether their proposed test method actually isolates the variable under test.
baseline: No baseline — current output contract accepts any test method that has a stopping condition.
success_threshold: ≥80% of generated hypotheses include explicit confound acknowledgment or controlled design when test method involves correlation claims
falsification_criteria: This hypothesis is FALSE if: (1) Adding validity checks causes >50% of hypothesis generation attempts to stall without resolution, (2) Practitioners say the challenge criteria feel like bureaucracy rather than clarification, (3) Well-designed experiments get flagged as invalid due to false positive triggers
